subject: Product Management
title: The Tyranny of the Dashboard
description: A fast breakdown of The Tyranny of the Dashboard for builders.

hook.text: A beautiful dashboard with 47 charts gives the illusion of insight while drowning teams in data they don't act on.

buildup.text: Teams build dashboards that track everything. Nobody checks them. When they do, nobody knows what action to take.

discovery.text: Effective dashboards track 3-5 metrics that directly connect to decisions the team makes weekly.

twist.text: The problem isn't lack of data — it's lack of questions. Start with 'what decision will this data inform?' and work backwards.

climax.text: A dashboard nobody acts on is decoration. A dashboard that changes behavior is a tool. Most dashboards are decoration.

punchline.text: If a chart doesn't change decisions, remove it from the dashboard.

quiz.question: What makes a product dashboard effective?
options: Tracking 3-5 metrics that directly connect to weekly decisions
Displaying as many metrics as possible
